title: Randomised controlled trial of yoga and bio-feedback in management of hypertension
headline: Randomised controlled trial of yoga and bio-feedback in management of hypertension
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined clinical research. 34 hypertensive patients were assigned at random either to six weeks' treatment by yoga relaxation methods with bio-feedback or to placebo therapy (general relaxation).
keyFindings: 34 hypertensive patients were assigned at random either to six weeks' treatment by yoga relaxation methods with bio-feedback or to placebo therapy (general relaxation).
Both groups showed a reduction in blood-pressure (from 168/100 to 141/84 mm.
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
